[PDF_PAGE 8 | score=2] pear. For example. the damage statistic of an avatar wield­ ing a sword +3 with a Strength of 19 would look this way: Omg ld8+3 (sword) +7. Most avalars have the ability to move only over land. However , some can also fly. swim. or burrow, often at the same rate that the avatar moves over land. In these cases, the additional movement mode is indicated by an abbreviation following the movement rate , like this: MV 15 SW. If the fly­ ing or swimming rate is different . the abbreviation will be preceded by the appropriate numbe r. In cases of flying ava­ tars , they are always assumed to have a maneuve rability class A. Often. avatars have special abilities in addition to thei r normal class ab
[PDF_PAGE 13 | score=1] Introduction Abbreviations The following abbreviations a re used in Legends k Lore. AUgnment abbreviations: Ig (lawful good); ng (neutral good); cg (chaotic goo d ); In (lawfu l neutral); en (chaotic neu­ tral); Ie (lawfu l evil); ne (neutral evil); ce (chaotic evil); g (good); e (evil) ; c (chaotic); n (t rue neutral) ; 1 (lawful). Deities' statistics: All deities will have their AD&Q® game statistics presented in the following format: Statistics: AL Ig; WAL any g; Aoe sun; SY hall of ligh!. [PDF_PAGE 25 | score=1] Raven (lesser god) .J Raven is one of the most powe rful of the .dJt9 many animal spirits . He professes to have jlJ' 'rI' created the world by pulling it up from be- ~ neath the waters. The validity of this claim remains In doubt . however , for he is well '-'- _____ J known as a trickster who resorts to any- thing losatisfy his ravenou s appelite . Though his true form is that of a large black raven , he can polymorph at will into many different form s, including a fox , jay, creven the moon . Role-playing Notes: Though generally good-hearted , the unpredictable Raven has a narrow range of concerns-and most of them have to do with food . Raven never answers ap­ peals for aid unless an offe
[PDF_PAGE 28 | score=1] American Indian Heroes American Indian Monsters Qagwaaz Qagwaaz is a powerful warrior who wanders the plains alone . captu ring wild horses and hunting buffalo solely for the sport of it. Ranger 12, Bard 5 Sir 18/52 Dex 17 Wis1 8 AC6 HP1S6 THAC07 Int 18 MR 10 % ALng fAT 312 Con 17 Cha 17 MV12 Omg 1d6 (club) + 3 Spells: 1) invisibility to animals, pass without Irace, ven­ triloquism ; 2) produce flame, messenger; 3) hold animal. He is a welcome guest in the camps of all the plains tribes, where he enjoys challenging the resident warriors 10 contesls of prowess and skill. In such contests, Qagwaaz relies only upon a wooden club and his great strength so that he does not injure his opponents to
[PDF_PAGE 145 | score=1] Tsukl· Yomi (intermediate god) Hachiman (intermediate god) r--o~-_-, Tsuki.Yomi was born when Izanagi washed his right eye after returning from the Land of the Dead. He is the god of the moon, whose function it is to count the passing of the months. In China, a hare is L-"_~"-_J often shown in the moon, and this sign has been incorporated into Tsuki-Yomi's symbol. along with a pun. In Japanese . Mochi-zuki means two things: the full moon , and 10 pound rice for cakes. Therefore , Tsuki- Vomi's symbol is a hare in the moon, pounding rice for cakes. As the counter of the months, T suki-Yomi has the power to control time. making it pass more slowly in one place than in another , stopping it alt
[PDF_PAGE 164 | score=1] Tyaa (intermediate god) Red God (intermediate god) Tyaa, is a force of evil and chaos. She ,----­ chooses to manifest her powers through L the actions of malicious birds. She has cre- ated an offshoot of the raven that is both smarter and stronge r than its ancestral cousin. It should be noted that. while she L _____ ~ prefers to employ the Birds of Tyaa, she is perfectly ca pable of using any carnivorous bird to do her will on Nehwon. The city of Lankhmar has always been her favorite because she has the most wo rshippers in this place. Tyaa's cult has been banned in Lankhmar because of its evil teachings and ways. This has only caused the movement to go underground and to othercilies. There
[PDF_PAGE 179 | score=1] Aeglr (intermediate god) Baldur (intermediate god) ,------, Although more closely related to the gi­ ants than either the Aesir or Ihe Vanir, Aegir is the god of the occan and personi­ fication of its strength for good or ill. He lives in a great castle on the bottom of the occan with his wife. Ran, and their nine daughters. Aegir is sometimes called "Alebrewer " because he often throws feasts for his friends, the Aesir. Aegir has the power to create or quell stor ms at sea. In his true form. he is a giant standing sixty feet tall. He has a long gray beard, and carrics a tree-sized club carved in the shape of a maiden which can deliver 4dlO points of damage to struc­ tures or beings. Role-pl
[PDF_PAGE 184 | score=1] Loki (Intermediate god) Tyr (Intermediate god) Loki is the god of mischief and strife. A sly, ,----- ~c:- vengeful trickster who is always causing trouble among the gods, he is tolerated in Asgard only because of the great services he has performed in the past , such as help­ ing to create Midgard and the wall guard- ing Asgard . Loki has the power to change his shape into anything . If any being , including other gods . look at Loki for more than three rounds, he can exert his innuence over them in the form of a suggestion spell with no saving throw. He also wears a pair of boots thai allow him to walk on waler , fly. and run at a movement rate of so. In his true form . Loki is a handsome y
